title: Carum tenerum
intro: Carum tenerum, also known as Ajwain, is a small, annual herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Middle East and India. The health benefits of Carum tenerum include its use as a digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it effective in relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Carum tenerum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Carum tenerum include teas, decoctions, and capsules, which are used to alleviate digestive issues and respiratory problems.
